subject: Water bill tripled this month — no explanation
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've been at 2218 Harvard Ave for eleven years and my water bill has never gone above $80. This January statement shows $247. We haven't had guests, haven't filled a pool, nothing unusual. I called the billing office on January 9th and was told it might be an estimated read. That's not an answer. Can someone please send an actual meter reader out so we can figure out what's going on?

subject: Water bill tripled this month — no explanation
body_clean: Ms. Marchetti, thank you for reaching out to the Mayor's office. I've pulled your account for 2218 Harvard Ave. Your November read was estimated due to access issues — our reader couldn't reach the pit. The January bill corrected for two billing cycles, which is what caused the spike. I'm scheduling a manual re-read for Tuesday the 14th. If actual consumption confirms normal usage, we'll issue an adjusted bill within five business days. I'll follow up once the re-read is complete.

subject: Received shutoff notice — bill was paid through autopay
body_clean: Good afternoon. I'm writing because I got a shutoff notice dated April 8th for my property at 3107 Bellwood Ave. My bank confirms that the autopay payment of $64.50 cleared on March 28th, nine days before the notice was even generated. This is the Village's error, not mine, and I'm asking that the shutoff be removed from my account immediately. I can forward the bank confirmation if needed.

subject: Received shutoff notice — bill was paid through autopay
body_clean: Mr. Tillis, I sincerely apologize. The March payment posted on the 28th as you said. The shutoff notice was generated before our system reconciled the autopay batch — it should not have gone out. I've flagged your account to cancel the shutoff action; your service will not be interrupted. Your account is in good standing. If you'd like written confirmation for your records, just reply and I'll send a letter on Village letterhead.

subject: Usage reading seems impossibly high — possible shared meter
body_clean: Hi Mayor — I'm a renter at 1402 Frederick Ave, Unit 2. My landlord forwarded a bill showing 28,000 gallons for June. There are two people in this apartment — no dishwasher, no irrigation. I've checked every faucet and toilet and nothing is running. Is it possible the meter is shared with the downstairs unit and they're billing us for the whole building? I'd appreciate someone investigating before my landlord tries to charge me for it.

subject: Requesting payment plan — unexpected medical bills
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've lived at 2709 Granville Ave for eight years and I've always paid on time. This fall I went through emergency surgery and the medical bills wiped out my savings. I currently owe $318 on my water account and cannot pay that in one shot. I'm asking if the Village has any kind of payment arrangement. I want to stay current and keep my service on — just need some flexibility while I recover.

subject: Requesting payment plan — unexpected medical bills
body_clean: Mr. Speights, the Village does offer a hardship payment plan for accounts with strong payment history, and yours qualifies. We can split your $318 balance into three monthly installments of $106, with the first due November 1st, provided future charges stay current. I'll email you a simple agreement to sign. Your service will not be interrupted as long as you remain on the plan. Call me at 708-555-0142 with any questions.

subject: Estimated bill for the fourth month in a row
body_clean: I am writing to the Mayor directly because I have called Water Billing three times and nothing has changed. My meter at 820 Morris Ave has now been estimated four billing cycles in a row. My bills have climbed from $55 to $91 and I have no idea if the estimates are accurate. I am a 73-year-old woman living alone and I do not use this much water. I need someone to actually come out and read my meter.

subject: Estimated bill for the fourth month in a row
body_clean: Mrs. Przybylski, thank you for writing and I'm sorry this dragged on so long. Four estimated reads in a row is not acceptable. I've asked Lorena Diaz in Water Billing to personally schedule a physical meter read at your home this week — she will call you first to confirm a time. If actual consumption is lower than what's been billed, you'll receive a credit on your next statement. You have my direct attention on this.

subject: Meter replaced but bill is still sky high
body_clean: Hi — I'm at 537 Englewood Ave. Public Works replaced my meter in September because it was reading high. I was told the new meter would fix the billing. My October bill just came in at $189, still way above my normal $60–70. Either the old meter's inflated reads weren't corrected in the system, or the new meter has the same problem. I've been patient for two months. Can someone audit what's going on with my account?

subject: Never received a bill — now facing a late fee
body_clean: Good morning Mayor. I moved to 2055 Monroe St in March. I never received a water bill — not by mail, not by email. I only found out I owed money when a late notice with a $25 fee arrived. I set up service in person at Village Hall and gave my correct mailing address. This late fee isn't fair when I never got the original bill. Please help me get this corrected.

subject: Never received a bill — now facing a late fee
body_clean: Ms. Holloway, the mailing address was entered with a transposition error at setup — 2505 instead of 2055 — so bills were going to the wrong address. Because this was our error, I've waived the $25 late fee and corrected your address in the system. Your statement will come to the right place going forward. I'd also recommend enrolling in e-billing as a backup; I can set that up for you if you reply with your email preference.

subject: Shutoff notice received while on payment plan
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — I need to flag something urgently. I entered a payment plan with Water Billing in November for my balance at 1228 St. Paul Ave. I've made every payment on time. This morning I got a shutoff notice for February 18th. I have receipts for all three installments. Something is broken in how payments are being tracked. Please have someone look at this today — I have a family at home and temperatures are in the single digits.

subject: Shutoff notice received while on payment plan
body_clean: Mr. Esparza, I reviewed your account immediately. All three payment plan payments are on record and you are current. The shutoff notice was generated in error by an automated batch that didn't read the plan exemption flag correctly. I've contacted our field crew — the February 18th shutoff action is cancelled and your service will not be interrupted. I'm very sorry for the alarm and will personally monitor your account through the end of the plan period.

subject: Usage spike while we were on vacation — meter running with no one home
body_clean: Hi, I'm reaching out about our bill at 418 Bohland Ave. My husband and I were in Florida for all of March — nobody was home and the main was shut off at the house valve. Our March bill still shows 11,400 gallons used. That's physically impossible. I suspect there may be a problem at the meter itself, possibly registering flow even when nothing is open. Can we get a meter test or inspection scheduled?

subject: Enrolled in e-billing over a year ago — still getting paper bills with fees
body_clean: Mayor — I enrolled in paperless billing over a year ago. I'm still getting paper statements every month with a $2.50 mailing fee itemized on each one. Over fourteen months that's $35 in fees I should not have been charged. I live at 1122 Mannheim Rd. I'd like a refund of the mailing fees and confirmation that my account is actually set to e-billing only going forward.

subject: Asking for bill relief after February water main break
body_clean: To the Mayor: there was a water main break on my block of 19th Ave in February. For about six hours pressure was surging and then dropping. My March bill shows significantly higher usage than any prior month and I did not change my habits. I want to ask whether the Village will consider adjusting bills for households on that block. It seems unfair to charge us full rate for conditions we didn't cause.

subject: Asking for bill relief after February water main break
body_clean: Mr. Steinberg, that's a fair question. I've asked Public Works to cross-reference the date and block of the February break against billing records for the affected addresses. If usage spikes correlate with the incident window, I'm inclined to offer an adjustment. I'll have a decision within a couple of weeks and will reach out to all affected households on 19th Ave. Thank you for raising this — it's exactly the kind of equity issue we should be looking at.

subject: Senior water rate discount applied incorrectly
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I applied for the senior water rate discount back in June when I turned 65. I submitted my ID and everything the office requested. My July, August, and September bills have all come in at the full residential rate. I'm spending about $14 more per month than I should be. I live at 724 St. Charles Rd. Please look into why the discount hasn't been applied and whether I'll be credited for the months that were billed at the wrong rate.

subject: Billing address still wrong after I updated it online
body_clean: Hi — I updated my mailing address online for my water account at 2620 Hirsch Ave because statements were going to my old place. The portal confirmed the change was saved. My last two bills have still gone to the wrong address — my previous neighbor texted me to say she's been receiving them. I'm afraid I'll fall behind without knowing it. Can someone verify the change went through and resend me the January and February statements?

subject: Final water bill after home sale doesn't match closing payoff
body_clean: Mayor, I closed on the sale of 1703 Washington Blvd on May 31st. The title company got a payoff figure from Water Billing of $47.10, which was collected at closing. Now I'm receiving a "final bill" for $89.40 — nearly double what was quoted. I no longer own this property and the new owner is confused too. Can someone clarify whether the closing payoff figure was calculated incorrectly, and who is actually responsible for the difference?

subject: Can't understand the tiered rate structure on my December bill
body_clean: Hello Mayor Okonkwo. My December water bill for 2245 Bellwood Ave breaks usage into three tiers with different rates but there's no explanation of how many gallons each tier covers or why the third tier is so much higher per unit. My total went from $74 last December to $98 this year even though my usage seemed about the same. Is there a rate sheet I can get? Did rates go up?

subject: New water account setup not confirmed — moving in July 2nd
body_clean: Hello, I'm purchasing 1530 Bellwood Ave and closing June 27th. I applied for new water service online three days ago and have not received a confirmation. I move in July 2nd. Can someone confirm the application was received and that water service will be active when I arrive? I don't want to show up to my new home without water.

subject: New water account setup not confirmed — moving in July 2nd
body_clean: Ms. Ochieng, congratulations on your new home. I located your application — it was flagged for manual review because the prior owner's account had a small open balance, which has since been resolved through closing proceeds. Your new account is cleared and service at 1530 Bellwood Ave will be fully active by June 28th, well before your July 2nd move-in. Welcome to Bellwood!

subject: Online payment portal down — bill due tomorrow
body_clean: I've been trying to pay my December water bill online since yesterday. Every time I enter my account number the portal gives me a service unavailable error. My bill for 3305 50th Ave is due December 15th and I don't want a late fee because your website is broken. Is there a phone number I can call to pay today? And please get the portal fixed.

subject: Meter read done in person — bill still shows estimated
body_clean: Hi. Someone from Public Works came to read the meter at 2114 Geneva Ave on March 6th — I was home and watched him write down the reading. My March bill still shows E for estimated and the number is way off from what I saw him record. It looks like the actual reading never made it into the billing system. Can you check whether it was entered?
